title: Hiking and Biking in Sedona
summary:
Our biggest stop on the way to Durango was in Sedona. We spent two nights at the dispersed camping area to the west of town. On our first night there, I went on a nice run up Bear Mountain while Helen wrapped up some work. Bear mountain was a great steep trail that reminded me of doing laps up Mount Sentinel in Missoula. On our second day, Helen and I hiked up Boynton Canyon to the Subway Cave, a cool rock formation that is reminiscent of a subway station. The cave was right next to some Native American ruins, which were exciting to see since I had just started the book 'House of Rain' by Craig Childs, which explores the history of the native people in the Southwest. On our last day, we went on a nice mountain bike ride on the way out of town.

-Colin
